channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Your cheat sheet to the teams headed to the World Cup semi-finals
item.description: We're at the final stretch of the World Cup. It feels like just yesterday, international fans were arriving in the U.S. and marveling at Buc-ee's and unlimited free soda.
item.content:encoded:
We're at the final stretch of the World Cup. It feels like just yesterday, international fans were arriving in the U.S. and marveling at Buc-ee's and unlimited free soda.

(Image credit: Justin Setterfield)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: Death toll from a Bangkok music bar fire rises to 30, dozens remain in hospital
item.description: More than 70 people were injured, with 24 of them still in critical condition, according to a statement by the Bangkok Metropolitan Administration.
item.content:encoded:
More than 70 people were injured, with 24 of them still in critical condition, according to a statement by the Bangkok Metropolitan Administration.

(Image credit: Sakchai Lalit)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The star-studded World Cup semifinals kick off with France vs. Spain
item.description: The expanded World Cup allowed FIFA to introduce a new seeding system that placed the world's top teams in opposite corners of the knockout bracket. Now, the semifinals are both heavyweight matchups.
item.content:encoded:
The expanded World Cup allowed FIFA to introduce a new seeding system that placed the world's top teams in opposite corners of the knockout bracket. Now, the semifinals are both heavyweight matchups.

(Image credit: Florencia Tan Jun, Molly Darlington, Richard Pelham, David Ramos)
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: Trump's HHS abandons threat to withhold Medicare and Medicaid funding over trans care
item.description: NPR has learned that the Department of Health and Human Services will not be finalizing its most aggressive attempt to end gender-affirming care for youth nationally.
item.content:encoded:
NPR has learned that the Department of Health and Human Services will not be finalizing its most aggressive attempt to end gender-affirming care for youth nationally.

(Image credit: Erik McGregor)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: South Carolina's governor names Lindsey Graham's sister to serve out his term
item.description: South Carolina's governor says he is appointing Darline Graham to finish the term of her late brother. Top Republicans, including President Trump, have coalesced around the pick.
item.content:encoded:
South Carolina's governor says he is appointing Darline Graham to finish the term of her late brother. Top Republicans, including President Trump, have coalesced around the pick.

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: Sam Gringlas

item.title: On foreign policy, Graham's influence may have been felt the most
item.description: Over the course of his three decades in Congress, Lindsey Graham was a major influence on Capitol Hill and throughout the world as one of the most vocal advocates for U.S. military might.
item.content:encoded: Over the course of his three decades in Congress, Lindsey Graham was a major influence on Capitol Hill and throughout the world as one of the most vocal advocates for U.S. military might.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt

item.title: The U.S. strikes Iran after Trump announces a renewed blockade and tolls in Hormuz
item.description: The U.S. and Iran are fighting for control of the Strait of Hormuz, threatening a return to all-out war after agreeing to a ceasefire last month.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. and Iran are fighting for control of the Strait of Hormuz, threatening a return to all-out war after agreeing to a ceasefire last month.

(Image credit: Razieh Poudat)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: States sue to stop Paramount-Warner Bros blockbuster merger
item.description: California is among the states suing to block Paramount from buying Warner Bros. Discovery in a Hollywood mega-merger that would unite some of the nation's largest movie studios and TV newsrooms.
item.content:encoded:
California is among the states suing to block Paramount from buying Warner Bros. Discovery in a Hollywood mega-merger that would unite some of the nation's largest movie studios and TV newsrooms.

(Image credit: Bloomberg via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: The U.S.-Iran ceasefire grows more distant. And, Congress faces a consequential week
item.description: The U.S. and Iran exchanged fire over the weekend amid tensions over the Strait of Hormuz. And, Congress returns from recess facing tight deadlines.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. and Iran exchanged fire over the weekend amid tensions over the Strait of Hormuz. And, Congress returns from recess facing tight deadlines.

(Image credit: Majid Saeedi)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Pythons' extreme biology may hold clues for treating human disease
item.description: These snakes can go for months without eating, grow and shrink the size of their hearts and jump start their metabolism on a dime.
item.content:encoded:
These snakes can go for months without eating, grow and shrink the size of their hearts and jump start their metabolism on a dime.

(Image credit: Ari Daniel for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel
